 {"id":"bd-3obms","title":"Improved Crossing Minimization (Sifting Algorithm)","description":"## Goal
Augment or replace the barycenter heuristic in diagram_layout.rs with the sifting algorithm for better edge crossing reduction. The existing barycenter approach is correct but produces suboptimal layouts for dense graphs (10+ nodes per layer).

## Background
- Current: barycenter_sort does bidirectional sweeps using neighbor position averages. O(n*E) per sweep, good results for sparse graphs.
- Sifting: for each node in a layer, try inserting it at every position and pick the one with minimum crossings. O(n^2 * E) per layer but finds significantly better solutions.
- The layout quality metrics already measure crossings — improvement is directly measurable.

## Implementation
1. Add sifting_sort() alongside barycenter_sort() in diagram_layout.rs.
2. For each layer, for each node: remove from position, count crossings at each alternate position, insert at best position.
3. Use the optimized O(1) crossing counter (the ref_member boolean array) from the barycenter fix.
4. Run sifting after barycenter (as a refinement pass) rather than as a replacement.
5. Budget-gated: sifting is more expensive; respect the computation budget.

## Expected Improvement
- 15-30% fewer crossings on graphs with 20+ nodes (based on graph drawing literature).
- No change for simple graphs (barycenter already optimal for small inputs).

## Files
- crates/ftui-extras/src/diagram_layout.rs (add sifting_sort, integrate with minimize_crossings)

## Tests
- Unit: sifting produces <= crossings compared to barycenter alone
- Property: sifting output is a valid permutation of the layer
- Benchmark: measure crossing reduction on 10 test graphs","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T15:29:48.481508990Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T18:37:04.535774898Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T18:37:04.535753317Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["layout","mermaid","optimization"],"comments":[{"id":791,"issue_id":"bd-3obms","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear claiming. Will implement the sifting algorithm as a refinement pass after barycenter sort in the crossing minimization pipeline.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:30:11Z"},{"id":792,"issue_id":"bd-3obms","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GoldBear: Implementation complete. Added:
1. count_crossings_pair() - counts crossings between a single adjacent layer pair
2. count_crossings_at() - counts crossings involving a specific layer (both adjacent pairs)
3. sifting_sort() - for each node in a layer, tries reinserting at every position and picks the one with minimum crossings. Budget-gated, only runs on layers with 3+ nodes
4. Integration in minimize_crossings() - sifting runs as a refinement pass after each barycenter sweep iteration, only when budget allows and crossings remain
5. 7 new tests: valid permutation, crossing reduction, budget respect, small layer noop, pipeline integration, dense graph improvement, pair counting consistency

All 505 ftui-extras tests pass, clippy clean.","created_at":"2026-02-05T18:36:59Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3r87","title":"Text Effects: Scanline / CRT Effects","description":"## Background
CRT/scanline effects create strong nostalgic retro appeal, perfect for terminal UIs. Simulates the look of old monitors applied to text.

## Implementation

Add TextEffect variant:
```rust
TextEffect::Scanline {
    intensity: f64,      // 0.0-1.0, how dark the scan lines (0.3 typical)
    line_gap: u8,        // Every Nth row dimmed (2 = every other row)
    scroll: bool,        // Lines scroll slowly for CRT feel
    scroll_speed: f64,   // Rows per second if scrolling
    flicker: f64,        // 0.0-1.0, random brightness variation
}
```

### Rendering:
```rust
fn apply_scanline(&self, color: PackedRgba, row: u16, time: f64) -> PackedRgba {
    // Determine if this row is a scanline
    let scroll_offset = if self.scroll { 
        (time * self.scroll_speed) as u16 
    } else { 
        0 
    };
    let is_scanline = (row + scroll_offset) % self.line_gap as u16 == 0;
    
    // Apply dimming
    let dim_factor = if is_scanline { 1.0 - self.intensity } else { 1.0 };
    
    // Apply flicker (random per-frame variation)
    let flicker_factor = 1.0 - self.flicker * random_for_frame(time);
    
    apply_alpha(color, dim_factor * flicker_factor)
}
```

### Note on Screen Effects:
Screen-wide effects like curvature, vignette, and barrel distortion are out of scope for text effects. Those would be terminal/screen-level post-processing, not text styling.

### Phosphor Glow:
For authentic CRT look, combine Scanline with a subtle Glow effect:
```rust
StyledText::new("RETRO")
    .effect(TextEffect::Scanline { intensity: 0.3, line_gap: 2, ... })
    .effect(TextEffect::Glow { color: green, intensity: 0.2, ... })
```

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_scanline_dims_every_nth: Correct rows affected
- test_scanline_intensity_applied: Color reduced by intensity
- test_scanline_scroll_moves_pattern: Pattern shifts with time
- test_scanline_flicker_varies: Different frames = different brightness
- test_scanline_gap_1_dims_all: Every row affected with gap=1
- test_scanline_gap_2_alternates: Every other row

## Visual Tests
- Snapshot multi-line text with scanlines, gap=2
- Snapshot same text at t=0 vs t=1 with scroll

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Scanline / CRT Effects
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-3sa7","title":"Demo Showcase: Snapshot/Time Travel Player","description":"## Background

FrankenTUI already embraces determinism (Buffer -> Diff -> Presenter). A natural superpower is "time travel":
- record frames / state snapshots
- replay deterministically
- inspect diffs and metadata

This screen turns the harness/snapshot infrastructure into a user-visible, jaw-dropping demo.

## UX / Core Interactions

- Timeline scrubber of recorded frames/snapshots.
- Playback controls: step back/forward, play/pause, jump to markers.
- Detail panel:
  - snapshot metadata (timestamp, terminal size, mode)
  - render stats (diff cell count, ANSI byte count)
  - optional: checksum/hash for integrity

Suggested keybindings:
- Space: play/pause
- Left/Right: step
- Home/End: first/last
- M: toggle markers

## Architecture Notes

- Use existing snapshot/time-travel capture mechanisms (ftui-harness / ftui-pty utilities).
- Store snapshots in a compact format:
  - buffer cells (or diffs)
  - metadata: size, mode, seed
- Optional integrity feature:
  - compute a hash chain over snapshots so replays are tamper-evident (useful for regression artifacts).

## Acceptance Criteria

- Can load a recorded snapshot set and scrub smoothly.
- Playback is deterministic under a fixed snapshot file.
- Displays at least one of:
  - per-frame diff cell count
  - per-frame ANSI byte count
  - checksum

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- "Isomorphism proof" view:
  - show that replay output matches original via checksums
  - surface the exact invariants being asserted

## Extreme Optimization

- Scrubbing must be responsive:
  - pre-index snapshots
  - avoid per-step allocations
  - enforce a budget for 10k-frame scrubs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:29.210554198Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:09.085370464Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T18:09:09.085352471Z","close_reason":"Completed Snapshot/Time Travel Player screen implementation: timeline scrubbing, playback controls, frame metadata display (change count, checksum, memory size), markers, recording mode, diagnostic panel. Integrated into demo-showcase app with all 14 snapshot tests passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","ux"],"comments":[{"id":445,"issue_id":"bd-3sa7","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Capture snapshots and play them back with scrub controls.
- Display metadata per snapshot (timestamp, labels).

Testing
- Unit tests for snapshot indexing and playback behavior.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs for scrub interactions.
","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:29Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-bo6w","title":"Define ProbeableCapability enum and probe sequences","description":"# Define ProbeableCapability enum and probe sequences

## Background
Part of Terminal Capability Auto-Upgrade (bd-3227). Define capabilities that can be probed at runtime.

## Technical Design

```rust
/// Capabilities that can be probed at runtime
#[derive(Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Debug)]
pub enum ProbeableCapability {
    /// TrueColor (24-bit RGB) support
    TrueColor,
    /// Synchronized output (mode 2026)
    SynchronizedOutput,
    /// OSC 8 hyperlinks
    Hyperlinks,
    /// Kitty keyboard protocol
    KittyKeyboard,
    /// Sixel graphics
    Sixel,
    /// iTerm2 inline images
    ItermImages,
}

impl ProbeableCapability {
    /// All probeable capabilities
    pub fn all() -> &'static [Self] {
        &[
            Self::TrueColor,
            Self::SynchronizedOutput,
            Self::Hyperlinks,
            Self::KittyKeyboard,
            Self::Sixel,
            Self::ItermImages,
        ]
    }
    
    /// Get probe sequence bytes
    pub fn probe_sequence(&self) -> &'static [u8] {
        match self {
            // DA2 - Device Attributes Level 2
            Self::TrueColor => b"\x1b[>c",
            // Query mode 2026
            Self::SynchronizedOutput => b"\x1b[?2026$p",
            // Query XTGETTCAP for hyperlinks
            Self::Hyperlinks => b"\x1bP+q485950\x1b\\",
            // Query keyboard mode
            Self::KittyKeyboard => b"\x1b[?u",
            // Query Sixel support via DA1
            Self::Sixel => b"\x1b[c",
            // iTerm2 specific query
            Self::ItermImages => b"\x1b]1337;ReportCellSize\x07",
        }
    }
    
    /// Default timeout for this probe
    pub fn timeout(&self) -> Duration {
        match self {
            // Quick probes
            Self::TrueColor | Self::SynchronizedOutput => Duration::from_millis(100),
            // May need more time
            Self::Hyperlinks | Self::KittyKeyboard => Duration::from_millis(200),
            // Slower responses typical
            Self::Sixel | Self::ItermImages => Duration::from_millis(300),
        }
    }
    
    /// Convert to general Capability for upgrade
    pub fn to_capability(&self) -> Capability {
        match self {
            Self::TrueColor => Capability::TrueColor,
            Self::SynchronizedOutput => Capability::SynchronizedOutput,
            Self::Hyperlinks => Capability::Hyperlinks,
            Self::KittyKeyboard => Capability::KittyKeyboard,
            Self::Sixel => Capability::Sixel,
            Self::ItermImages => Capability::Images,
        }
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All 6 probe capabilities defined
- [ ] Probe sequences are correct per terminal specs
- [ ] Timeouts are conservative but practical
- [ ] Mapping to general Capability works","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:23:32.604035018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T20:43:39.362989807Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T20:43:39.362925626Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-bo6w","depends_on_id":"bd-3227","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-c6x0","title":"Implement Input Macro Recording tests","description":"# Implement Input Macro Recording tests

## Background
Part of Input Macro Recording (bd-17ed). Tests ensure macros record and replay correctly.

## Unit Tests
- record_captures_all_events: Key, mouse, paste events all captured
- timing_preserved_in_recording: delay_ms reflects actual timing
- playback_fires_events_in_order: Events replay in recorded order
- save_and_load_roundtrip: Saved macro loads identically
- file_format_is_human_readable: Output is valid YAML/JSON

## Edge Case Tests
- empty_macro_handles_gracefully: Zero events in macro works
- very_long_macro_scales: 10K+ events load/save efficiently
- unicode_event_content_preserved: Emoji and CJK characters in paste events
- rapid_fire_events_captured: Events <1ms apart handled correctly
- corrupted_file_detected: Invalid YAML returns clear error
- version_mismatch_handled: Old macro format gracefully upgraded or rejected

## Property Tests
- any_event_sequence_survives_roundtrip: Record then save/load preserves all events
- timing_is_monotonic: delay_ms values are non-negative

## Integration Tests
- record_form_submission_and_replay: Capture real form interaction
- replay_produces_same_model_state: Model after replay matches model after manual input
- macro_works_with_ProgramSimulator: Integration with deterministic testing
- resize_during_playback_handled: Terminal resize mid-macro works

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] All edge case tests pass
- [ ] Property tests find no counterexamples
- [ ] Integration tests verify real interaction capture","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:35.601421019Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T19:44:47.869528249Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T19:44:47.869509314Z","close_reason":"18 input_macro tests implemented in bd-17ed covering: recording, timing, playback order, empty macros, mixed events, determinism, quit handling, reset, step/replay_all/replay_until.  {"id":"bd-cw8","title":"Implement Markdown renderer","description":"PHASE 7 EXTRAS - MARKDOWN RENDERER

## Background
Markdown rendering is essential for agent harness UIs that display documentation, help text, and AI-generated content.

## Technical Requirements

### Supported Markdown Elements
- Headers (h1-h6) with styled text
- Bold, italic, strikethrough, code
- Links (rendered as OSC 8 hyperlinks if supported)
- Code blocks with syntax highlighting (optional)
- Lists (ordered and unordered)
- Blockquotes
- Horizontal rules
- Tables (basic)

### Parser Choice
- Use pulldown-cmark for parsing (battle-tested)
- Convert events to ftui Text/Spans

### Renderer Structure

pub struct MarkdownRenderer {
    theme: MarkdownTheme,
    code_theme: Option<SyntaxTheme>,
    link_style: Style,
    max_width: Option<u16>,
}

pub struct MarkdownTheme {
    h1: Style,
    h2: Style,
    h3: Style,
    // ...
    code_inline: Style,
    code_block: Style,
    blockquote: Style,
    link: Style,
}

impl MarkdownRenderer {
    pub fn render(&self, markdown: &str) -> Text {
        // Parse markdown
        // Convert to styled spans
        // Handle line breaks and wrapping
    }
    
    pub fn render_to_buffer(&self, markdown: &str, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        // Render directly to buffer with area constraints
    }
}

### Widget Integration

pub struct Markdown<'a> {
    content: &'a str,
    renderer: MarkdownRenderer,
}

impl Widget for Markdown<'_> {
    fn render(self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        self.renderer.render_to_buffer(self.content, area, buf);
    }
}

### Syntax Highlighting Integration

    // Use syntect or tree-sitter
}

- [ ] Nested lists

- [ ] Themeable rendering
- [ ] OSC 8 links (when supported)

 {"id":"bd-pt0dv","title":"Mermaid Parser Core (Tokenizer + AST)","description":"Background\n- We must parse Mermaid fenced blocks into a deterministic AST.\n- Mermaid syntax varies by diagram type; a shared tokenizer + AST core prevents drift.\n\nScope\n- Implement tokenizer: keywords, identifiers, arrows, brackets, directives, strings, comments.\n- Parse Mermaid init directives (%%{init: {...}}%%) into AST nodes.\n- Parse comments/annotations and ignore or retain for diagnostics.\n- Build AST for all supported diagram types (flowchart, sequence, state, gantt, class, ER, mindmap, pie).\n- Parse subgraph/cluster blocks and diagram direction directives.\n- Parse styling directives (classDef/class/style/linkStyle) as AST nodes.\n- Parse link/click directives into metadata (even if degraded at render).\n- Robust error reporting with source spans (line/col) and expected tokens.\n- Error recovery: resynchronize at line boundaries to continue AST build when possible.\n- Deterministic normalization of whitespace + line endings.\n\nConsiderations\n- Avoid parser generators unless already used; keep dependencies minimal.\n- Avoid allocations in hot loops (use slices/indices).\n- Parser must never panic on malformed input.\n\nTests / Validation\n- Unit tests for lexing + parsing per diagram type.\n- Golden AST fixtures for canonical samples.\n- Error-case tests asserting span + error kind.\n- Seeded fuzz tests for tokenizer + parser to ensure no panics.\n\nSuccess Criteria\n- AST captures nodes, edges, labels, directives, and diagram type.\n- Parser errors are structured and location-aware; recovery yields partial AST when possible.\n- Parser returns identical AST for semantically equivalent whitespace.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RubyCompass","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:38:02.271400086Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T21:25:39.069074956Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T21:25:39.069053856Z","close_reason":"Mermaid core parser: directives/comments/subgraph/style/link + error recovery + tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
